data.title: Proficient Auto Logistics beats top-line and bottom-line estimates
data.description:
• Proficient Auto Logistics press release (NASDAQ:PAL): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.28 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $106.61M (+5.8% Y/Y) beats by $4.92M.
  • Adjusted Operating Ratio of 91.8% compared to 92.7% in 2023.
  • Total Units delivered of 507,712 in 2024, an increase of 10.5%.

data.title: Monopar Therapeutics to implement 1-for-5 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Monopar Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MNPR) said it will effect a 1-for-5 reverse stock split of its outstanding shares of common stock.
  • The company said that the reverse stock split is an effort to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.

data.description:

  • Revenue of C$213.33M (+2.1% Y/Y).

data.title: Bread Financial to partially repurchase $237.6 million of convertible notes
data.description:
• Bread Financial (NYSE:BFH) said it has entered into separate repurchase agreements for the partial repurchase of $237.6 million aggregate principal amount of its 4.25% convertible senior notes due 2028.
  • The repurchase is for an aggregate price of ~$350 million.
  • After the closing of the repurchases, ~ $78.6 million principal amount of the Notes will remain outstanding, from an initial issued principal balance of $316.25 million.

data.title: Akamai rises after Q2 beat, Craig-Hallum upgrades on organic growth acceleration in 2025
data.description:
Akamai Technologies' (NASDAQ:AKAM) stock jumped about 5% premarket on Friday after second quarter results and outlook exceeded expectations and Craig-Hallum upgraded the stock to Buy.

The firm has a $125 price target on the shares of Akamai — which provides cloud computing, security, and content delivery services.

The analysts noted that a mix shift to higher growth businesses has set Akamai up well for organic acceleration and rising margins in 2025. They added that Akamai is the largest Content Delivery Network, or CDN, and cloud optimization services provider in the U.S., delivering dynamic website, mobile and video content for thousands of enterprise customers through a distributed network of over 200,000 servers globally.

The analysts added that second quarter revenue was slightly ahead ($979.58M versus consensus $977M) as was EPS ($1.58 compared to $1.53 consensus). Top line growth of 2% year-over-year organic appears poised to bottom in the third quarter and accelerate into mid to upper single digits in 2025.

Non-Delivery portions of the business look to be on track to climb from 60% of the mix to about 75% of the revenue mix by the end of 2025. Given these portions grew 17% in the quarter, while delivery declined 18%, this mix shift is the story, according to the analysts.

Craig-Hallum noted that as we move into 2025, top line organic growth is highly likely to accelerate, possibly hitting high single/low double digits by the fourth quarter of 2025. At the same time, the analysts believe forward leverage is likely to be more than currently embedded and believe we will see improving non-GAAP operating margins as well.

The analysts added that despite a challenging environment and decelerating organic growth across business units, the company raised annual revenue guidance by $5M at the midpoint and annual EPS guidance by $0.10 at the midpoint.

This was an organic uptick, as the Noname acquisition was closed in late June, but had already been contemplated in guidance. Guidance for the third quarter of 2024 came in bracketing consensus for revenue and ahead of consensus on the bottom, reflecting the overall strength in the profitability guide, as per the analysts.

For the third quarter, Akamai expects earnings per share of $1.56 to $1.62, with a midpoint of $1.59, versus the estimate of $1.56. The Cambridge, Mass.-based company expects revenue of $988M to $1.01B, with a midpoint of $999M, which is just above the estimate of $998.7M.

For the full year 2024, the company expects revenue to be between $3.97B and $4.01B, with a midpoint at $3.99B, versus consensus of $3.99B. Meanwhile, non-GAAP EPS was forecast in the range of $6.34 to $6.47, with midpoint at $6.405, versus $6.31 consensus.

Akamai Technologies' (AKAM) has a Hold rating at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, which consistently beats the market. Meanwhile, the Seeking Alpha authors' (2 authors in total) average rating is more positive with a Buy, and so is the average Wall Street analysts' rating, Buy.

data.title: Berry declares $0.12 fixed dividend
data.description:
• Berry (NASDAQ:BRY) declared $0.12/share fixed quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • The company also declared a variable dividend of $0.05 per share.
  • Payable Aug. 20; for shareholders of record Aug. 12; ex-div Aug. 12.
  • See BRY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Altus Group Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.45, revenues of C$206.7M
data.description:
• Altus Group press release (OTCPK:ASGTF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.45.
  • Consolidated revenues were C$206.7 million, up 0.7%.
  • Profit (loss) was C$2.3 million, compared to C$11.9 million.
  • Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA was C$36.8 million, down 17.6%.
  • Net cash related to operating activities was C$39.8 million, up 83.5%, and Free Cash Flow was C$37.5 million, up 96.4%.
